what_it_does: Implements the Concurrent Cognition engine: it discovers file-based registries, runs declared roles against a resident model, resolves multi-scheme addressed inputs, dispatches concurrent role waves and item fans, supports jury/panel judgment, cross-unit reduce joins, over-context chunk-and-compose, and executes saved multi-step cascades through a run://-addressed store.
observations: Heavy reliance on file-discovered registries discovered fresh on each call (roles, skills, contexts, guides, generation policies, decisions, decision subtypes).
run_role supports two axes: declared input_addresses and op (generate/embed), with a default path that preserves prior behavior.
A generation-policy ladder escalates repetition_penalty when finish_reason=='length', reading ladder values from the registry.
Think-control routes to ollama native transport when the model id contains no '/'.

resolve_address dispatches many schemes (run, cas, blob, image, skill, context, guide, session, cap, board, clone, mind, vi-vision, decision, extraction) and uses a BARE_NAME sentinel for bare context keys.
SlotBudget and model_context_window read from ops/services.json; a process-wide VramGate singleton bounds concurrency.
run_swarm uses a ThreadPoolExecutor capped at swarm_slots, writes per-role run:// outputs, and emits one batched rollup per wave.
run_items inverts the axis (1 role × N units) and distinguishes fatal resolution failures from recorded per-unit processing failures.
run_chunked splits over-context text, maps chunks via run_items, composes via run_reduce, and fails loud if any chunk failed or was skipped.
run_jury and run_panel provide self-consistency and perspective-diverse judgment primitives with deterministic verdict rules.
run_reduce joins map outputs by role synthesis, pure rule, or cosine clustering; reduce rules are single-sourced in REDUCE_RULES.
run_cascade executes saved ActionRegistry declarations by selecting step primitives, threading outputs via run://, persisting each step, and emitting one op.run per step.
The chat_parts_spike proving spike and concurrency_probe remain alongside production G1+ code.
standouts: The scheme-dispatching address resolver (resolve_address) is a central spine connecting run:// and more than a dozen other address kinds to existing resolvers.
run_cascade closes the gap between validated action declarations and the engine primitives, with explicit step-kind selection and per-step op.run indexing.
run_role's input/op axes, resolved slots, and generation-policy ladder make it a multi-mode execution primitive while keeping defaults byte-identical.
run_items and run_chunked introduce per-unit resilience and load-bearing fail-loud semantics for over-context processing.
conventions: Follows registry-is-truth, fail-loud, reuse-don't-parallel, additive/behavior-preserving change, and driver-not-agent (no resolve/approve/dispatch).
Uses lazy imports inside resolver branches to keep the module-load graph clean.
Uses a module-level singleton for the global VramGate.
Extensive inline documentation and long docstrings explain design rationale and flagged follow-ups.
Breaks single-responsibility: one module mixes role execution, address resolution, concurrency primitives, chunking, jury/panel, reduce, and cascade execution.
Uses a large central if/elif switch for scheme dispatch rather than a pluggable resolver registry.
concerns: The module is very large and mixes spike, primitive, resolver, and cascade-runner responsibilities, creating cohesion and review burden.
resolve_address is a central bottleneck: adding a new scheme requires editing this file and its growing lazy-import branches.
run_cascade has deep per-step-kind conditionals with duplicated boilerplate, making it hard to trace step threading.
Some numeric defaults remain despite registry-driven claims (DEFAULT_RESERVE_R, DEFAULT_ROLE_CTX_TOKENS, CHARS_PER_TOKEN) and are used as fallbacks.
The generation-policy ladder's repetition_penalty may not reach vLLM because the transport does not yet forward it (documented as a cross-lane follow-up).
Many nested helper functions and classes are defined inside public functions, complicating testing and introspection.
notes: This is the company's concurrent cognition driver: it runs declared roles, resolves addressed inputs, dispatches bounded concurrent waves, and executes multi-step cascades, all wired through a run://-addressed store.
questions: Where are RoleRegistry, SkillRegistry, and the other registry classes defined, and what is their exact contract?
How does the store implement head/get_content/set_ref/put_content, and is it safe under concurrent writes?
What is the full shape of a Role object (e.g., can_fire, is_jury, verdict_rule, prompt_slot, schema_slot, thinking)?
How are generation policies and decision subtypes structured in their registry files?
What is the intended relationship between this module and runtime/scheduler.py (the serial driver)?
How do Suite.run_cascade and the MCP wrapper inject resolve_role, retrieve_fn, check_resolver, and panel_resolver?
What happens when a run_reduce cluster step has no embed_fn and the local embedder is down?
How are chunking token estimates validated against the actual tokenizer?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is the Concurrent Cognition G0 proving spike and G1 production driver. Actual content matches that but has grown into a broad engine surface that also owns address resolution, cascade execution, jury/panel, and chunking — slightly beyond a pure cognition driver.
apparent_themes: concurrency bounded by live hardware registry
intention_signals: Make cognition fully declarative and addressable: every role, skill, context, decision, and policy is file-discovered data; computation flows through resolved run:// addresses; concurrency is bounded by the live services registry; failures are loud; and saved cascades chain primitives into reusable computations.
novelty: An extensive scheme-dispatching address resolver and a cascade executor that turns validated declarations into chained engine primitives with per-step op.run indexing; also an over-context chunk-and-compose tier and per-unit resilience in item fans.
contribution: A unified engine surface for running declared roles concurrently, resolving multi-scheme addressed inputs, reducing and joining unit outputs, judging via jury/panel, and executing saved cascades, all through a run://-addressed store with fail-loud semantics.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/cognition.py is the concurrent cognition engine that discovers file-based registries, runs roles via a resident model, resolves multi-scheme addressed inputs, dispatches concurrent role waves and item fans, supports jury/panel judgment, cross-unit reduce joins, over-context chunk-and-compose, and executes saved multi-step cascades through a run://-addressed store.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a declarative, address-resolved, fail-loud concurrency substrate for chaining role executions into staged turns, swarms, juries, reduces, and cascades without hardcoded control flow.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based role registry from roles/*.py.
symbols.description: Returns a fresh RoleRegistry by scanning the roles directory; used by the spike and cascade runner to obtain canonical Role definitions.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based skill registry from skills/*.py.
symbols.description: Returns a fresh SkillRegistry; skill://<id> addresses resolve through this registry to retrieve declared instruction content.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based guide registry from guides/*.py.
symbols.description: Returns a fresh GuideRegistry; guide://<id> addresses resolve to narrative how-to content, the learner-facing second face of skills.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based context registry from contexts/*.py.
symbols.description: Returns a fresh ContextRegistry; context://<id> addresses resolve to declared context blobs.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based decision registry from decisions/*.py.
symbols.description: Returns a fresh DecisionRegistry; decision://<frame>/<id> resolves pending decision rows from this registry, while state is composed separately from take marks.

symbols.does: Maps a decision subtype to a generation-policy id.
symbols.description: Looks up a decision's subtype in the decision_subtypes registry and returns the associated explanation_policy id, or None if absent; used by the explanation path to set sampling regime and prompt framing.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based generation-policy registry.
symbols.description: Returns a fresh GenerationPolicyRegistry from generation_policies/*.py; run_role(policy=<id>) reads repetition-penalty ladder regimes from here.

symbols.does: Returns a role's declared input address list.
symbols.description: Reads role.spec.input_addresses if present; absent or empty returns an empty list, which triggers the default utterance-only input path.

symbols.does: Lists declared non-utterance inputs actually available this call.
symbols.description: Filters _declared_inputs to entries other than 'utterance' that are present in ctx or are addressable strings with a registered scheme when a store is supplied; controls whether run_role takes the default or composed input path.

symbols.does: Checks whether this call uses the legacy single-utterance input path.
symbols.description: Returns True when no extra declared inputs are supplied, ensuring run_role frames the input exactly as before the input-axis changes.

symbols.does: Resolves one declared input name to its value.
symbols.description: For run:// or other registered-scheme addresses it uses the canonical resolver with a required store; for bare names it reads ctx[name]; missing values raise.

symbols.does: Builds the raw text an embed role embeds.
symbols.description: Returns ctx['utterance'] when no extra inputs are supplied; otherwise concatenates each supplied declared input as 'name: value' lines.

symbols.does: Opt-in gated load of the embedder model.
symbols.description: Delegates to ops/cli/capabilities.ensure_resident to make the configured embedder resident before an embed op; returns structured result and can surface swap-approval asks.

symbols.does: Fires a single role request against the resident model and returns validated JSON.
symbols.description: The core primitive: selects generate or embed op, resolves input and prompt/schema/thinking slots, applies optional generation-policy repetition-penalty ladder, and returns the role's validated output dict; defaults preserve prior behavior.

symbols.does: Persists corpus embeddings into projection spaces.
symbols.description: Groups records by embeddable projection and delegates to store.vector_index.build_index, reusing the existing batch embed-and-put_vector path so find_relations can read them end-to-end.

symbols.does: Declared-data rule deciding whether to inject a recalled snippet.
symbols.description: Evaluates the INJECTION_RULE_AST via the runtime/rules engine over resolved recall and ground outputs; returns {inject, snippet, reason} for the staged turn.

symbols.does: Dataclass capturing one 2-part staged turn.
symbols.description: Records turn metadata, focus output, part timestamps, role finish order, run:// addresses, resolved values, and routing decision produced by chat_parts_spike.

symbols.does: Proves the 2-part staged turn mechanism.
symbols.description: Runs focus, emits Part 1 immediately, fires recall and ground concurrently, writes their outputs to run:// addresses, applies the injection rule after joining, and yields Part 2 enriched or base.

symbols.does: Concurrent worker that runs one wave role and writes its run:// ref.
symbols.description: Inside chat_parts_spike, calls run_role, stores the output via put_content/set_ref, and records the role's finish order under a lock.

symbols.does: Generator yielding Part 1 then Part 2 of the staged turn.
symbols.description: Yields the immediate base-context Part 1, joins the wave, resolves addresses, applies injection_rule, and yields the enriched or base Part 2.

symbols.does: Measures N concurrent run_role calls against the resident pool.
symbols.description: Spawns N threads running the same role, records completion count, errors, wall time, and latency percentiles to probe current resident concurrency behavior.

symbols.does: Single probe worker measuring one run_role latency.
symbols.description: Runs run_role once, catches exceptions, and appends latency or error under a lock.

symbols.does: Computes a latency percentile from sorted samples.
symbols.description: Helper returning the p-th percentile value from the sorted latencies list, or None if empty.

symbols.does: Dataclass holding the computed request-concurrency budget.
symbols.description: Stores max_num_seqs, reserve slots, per-role context size, KV pool tokens, main context tokens, swarm slot count, and provenance source.

symbols.does: Computes slot budget from live ops/services.json.
symbols.description: Reads the chat-4b service config, derives seq and KV bounds, and returns a SlotBudget; fails loud on missing service or max_num_seqs.

symbols.does: Returns the process-wide VramGate singleton.
symbols.description: Creates a VramGate sized to the full resident seq-cap once, then reuses it; the swarm sub-pool ensures reserve slots remain for the main stream.

symbols.does: Reads a value from a run:// address via the store.
symbols.description: Canonical resolver using store.head followed by store.get_content; raises on unresolved refs unless on_missing is declared.

symbols.does: Sentinel class for bare ctx-key names.
symbols.description: Returned by resolve_address for inputs without a scheme, signaling the caller to read the value from the supplied context dict.

symbols.does: String representation of the BARE_NAME sentinel.
symbols.description: Returns the literal '<BARE_NAME: read from ctx>'.

symbols.does: Scheme-dispatching resolver for any address.
symbols.description: Materializes <turn> templates, then dispatches run://, cas://, blob://, image://, skill://, context://, guide://, session://, cap://, board://, clone://, mind://, vi-vision://, decision://, extraction:// to existing resolvers; bare names return BARE_NAME; unknown schemes raise.

symbols.does: Dataclass for one role's per-wave run record.
symbols.description: Captures role_id, output run:// address, ok flag, latency ms, and optional error string for inclusion in the batched rollup.

symbols.does: Dataclass capturing one concurrent swarm wave.
symbols.description: Holds turn_id, resolved role outputs, run:// addresses, per-role RoleRun records, finish order, wall time, summed role time, and budget provenance.

symbols.does: Dispatches a concurrent wave of independent role runs.
symbols.description: Builds a ready-set, runs each role through a ThreadPoolExecutor bounded by budget.swarm_slots under the global VramGate, writes each output to run://<turn>/<role>, emits one batched rollup, and reads resolved values back.

symbols.does: Single swarm worker running one role and storing its output.
symbols.description: Acquires a VramGate slot, calls run_role, persists the validated output to the role's run:// address, and returns a RoleRun.

symbols.does: Dataclass capturing one run_items fan result.
symbols.description: Holds per-unit output addresses, resolved values, OK run records, finish order, declared-skipped units, and processing-failed units.

symbols.does: Fans one role over N input units concurrently.
symbols.description: Resolves or treats each item as a literal, builds per-unit context, runs the role under the global gate, writes each output to run://<turn>/<role>/<i>, emits one rollup, and returns OK units; resolution failures are fatal, processing failures are recorded per unit.

symbols.does: Exception class marking a fatal resolution failure.
symbols.description: Wraps an address-resolution exception so the barrier can distinguish it from a per-unit processing failure and re-raise it after the rollup.

symbols.does: Constructor storing the original cause.
symbols.description: Accepts the causing exception and forwards its string representation.

symbols.does: Resolves one input unit to the role's primary input.
symbols.description: Classifies strings starting with a registered scheme as addresses resolved via resolve_address; everything else is a literal used as-is.

symbols.does: Single run_items worker.
symbols.description: Resolves the unit, optionally applies unit_ctx templates, calls run_role, writes the output to the positional run:// address, and returns a RoleRun or None for skipped units.

symbols.does: Reads the model's max_model_len from ops/services.json.
symbols.description: Returns the context-window token count for the configured service; fails loud if the registry lacks it.

symbols.does: Computes per-chunk character budget from a token window.
symbols.description: Subtracts output and prompt token reservations, applies a margin, and converts to characters; raises on non-positive budgets.

symbols.does: Splits text into context-sized chunks.
symbols.description: Greedy packing on paragraph/line boundaries with hard char fallback for oversized pieces; deterministic and never emits empty chunks.

symbols.does: Dataclass capturing one chunk-and-compose result.
symbols.description: Records whether chunking occurred, chunk count, chunk char budget, map addresses, composed output, compose mode, and wall time.

symbols.does: Processes over-context text by split-map-compose.
symbols.description: Fits short text through a single run_role; for long text chunks it via chunk_text, maps the role over chunks with run_items, fails loud on any failed/skipped chunk, and composes the chunk outputs with run_reduce.

symbols.does: Dataclass capturing one jury run.
symbols.description: Holds turn_id, role_id, resolved draw values, per-draw addresses, content signatures, deterministic verdict, and wall time.

symbols.does: Runs N varied draws of a jury role and computes a deterministic verdict.
symbols.description: Verifies the role is a jury with a verdict_rule, fires N concurrent draws at temperature>0 to distinct run:// addresses, reads them back, and applies the pure verdict function.

symbols.does: Single jury draw worker.
symbols.description: Acquires a VramGate slot, runs the role, and writes the draw to its distinct run:// address with index.

symbols.does: Runs a perspective-diverse panel of different roles.
symbols.description: Fires one temperature-0 run per seat role, requires each output to carry a bool grounded field, and returns a deterministic quorum verdict; failed seats are recorded as dissent.

symbols.does: Reads a set of run:// map outputs in stable order.
symbols.description: Sorts addresses by unit_id, resolves each via resolve_run_ref, supports declared on_missing='skip', and returns (unit_id, address, value) tuples.

symbols.does: Dataclass capturing one cross-unit reduce.
symbols.description: Records turn_id, join mode, read-back inputs, skipped units, joined output, wall time, and mode-specific detail.

symbols.does: Pure reduce rule tallying verify-lens verdicts.
symbols.description: Returns fail if any input verdict is fail, green only if all non-empty inputs are pass, otherwise flag; surfaces missing-verdict indices.

symbols.does: Factory for tally-by:<field> reduce rules.
symbols.description: Returns a pure function that groups input dicts by a named field and counts occurrences, with missing fields counted visibly.

symbols.does: The generated tally-by rule callable.
symbols.description: Counts inputs by the configured field and returns {tally_by, counts, n}.

symbols.does: Resolves a reduce rule name to its pure callable.
symbols.description: Looks up static names in REDUCE_RULES or builds a tally-by:<field> rule on demand; returns None for unknown names so callers fail loud.

symbols.does: Joins a set of run:// addresses into one output.
symbols.description: Reads back the addressed values, then dispatches to role synthesis, pure rule application, or cosine embedding cluster; emits one batched rollup and returns a ReduceResult.

symbols.does: Renders a unit value as embeddable text.
symbols.description: Returns strings unchanged and dicts as stable sorted JSON for cluster embedding.

symbols.does: Embeds units and groups them by cosine nearness.
symbols.description: Uses the existing complete_embeddings path or an injected embed_fn, then greedily clusters units whose representative cosine meets the threshold; deterministic on sorted unit ids.

symbols.does: Selects the engine primitive for a cascade step.
symbols.description: Honors an explicit kind field or derives it from op and fan flags; fails loud on unknown kinds.

symbols.does: Executes a saved multi-step cascade declaration end-to-end.
symbols.description: For each step selects a primitive (role/items/reduce/retrieve/check/jury/panel), threads prior outputs into current inputs via run:// addresses, persists each step's output, and emits one op.run per step; returns step records and final output/address.
